title: End of Eva TV Spot
wikitext:
[]: End of Eva TV Spot             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Mon Apr 28, 2014 8:59 pm Found this little gem on youtube the other day [url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4HALL5-PZu4[/url] Probably the best representation of what the movie was going to be like for the fans back then. Are there anymore like these, because I'm genuinely interested in how they marketed this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:15 pm ...I would watch the hell out of that movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:21 pm Pretty much sums up the whole film

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:40 pm one of the best teasers of all time. Short, to the point, and it gets across the feeling and tone of the film brilliantly.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Apr 28, 2014 10:54 pm There are two version of that. The other ends with the sound of ocean waves lapping up against the shore in stead of giggles. I am also envious of these TV spots in every conceivable way. I’d love to construct a TV spot like this for one of my darker film projects. (I just might too.)

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Apr 29, 2014 12:25 am My favourite is the live-action one with '夢のスキマ' [Yume no Sukima] playing. Sends shivers down my spine every time. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ki7WIODbAw

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 29, 2014 12:48 am That was a TV spot??? Well, I'll be. It's at the beginning of the Manga DVDs. And yes, it's amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by Wanderlust » Tue Apr 29, 2014 4:44 am Between that TV spot and the trailer Blue Monday mentioned, I'm fully convinced EoE was the most brilliantly marketed film ever.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Apr 29, 2014 6:26 am Well, I love the Trailer, but the TV-spot strikes me as unintentionally funny.

[]: Re: End of Eva TV Spot             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Apr 29, 2014 6:44 am ApolloIVtv wrote: Found this little gem on youtube the other day [url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4HALL5-PZu4[/url] Probably the best representation of what the movie was going to be like for the fans back then. Are there anymore like these, because I'm genuinely interested in how they marketed this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Apr 29, 2014 9:55 am That was fairly indicative of the movie's contents.﻿ Also, lol, that random little Rei in the life action one. "In case all the artsiness confused you, this ad is for the movie that will have this chick in it"

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri May 02, 2014 1:47 pm Have seen it numerous times and yet it gets me every time.....

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri May 02, 2014 3:24 pm Kendrix wrote: Also, lol, that random little Rei in the life action one.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri May 02, 2014 3:33 pm The really scary thing is that this is more than just a rapid-fire slideshow of creepy moments backed up with mind rending screaming . I have a theory that this is actually a shot of Shinji's mind during Instrumentality. He was exposed to the combined mind of all humanity (which would explain the other viewpoints- they're other people's memories he's being forced to relive) and the screaming is of course his own as his mind is shredded into a thousand agonized shards that can never be repaired. We didn't just see a teaser. We saw the complete obliteration of a human being, leaving only a wretched husk of a thing that knows only pain, having seen beyond the end, wrought by his own hands.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sat May 03, 2014 7:11 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: That was a TV spot??? Well, I'll be. It's at the beginning of the Manga DVDs. And yes, it's amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Mon May 05, 2014 3:42 am Squigsquasher wrote: The really scary thing is that this is more than just a rapid-fire slideshow of creepy moments backed up with mind rending screaming . I have a theory that this is actually a shot of Shinji's mind during Instrumentality. He was exposed to the combined mind of all humanity (which would explain the other viewpoints- they're other people's memories he's being forced to relive) and the screaming is of course his own as his mind is shredded into a thousand agonized shards that can never be repaired. We didn't just see a teaser. We saw the complete obliteration of a human being, leaving only a wretched husk of a thing that knows only pain, having seen beyond the end, wrought by his own hands.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon May 05, 2014 5:56 am Squigsquasher wrote: The really scary thing is that this is more than just a rapid-fire slideshow of creepy moments backed up with mind rending screaming . I have a theory that this is actually a shot of Shinji's mind during Instrumentality. He was exposed to the combined mind of all humanity (which would explain the other viewpoints- they're other people's memories he's being forced to relive) and the screaming is of course his own as his mind is shredded into a thousand agonized shards that can never be repaired. We didn't just see a teaser. We saw the complete obliteration of a human being, leaving only a wretched husk of a thing that knows only pain, having seen beyond the end, wrought by his own hands.

[]: Re: End of Eva TV Spot             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon May 05, 2014 7:48 pm ApolloIVtv wrote: Found this little gem on youtube the other day [url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4HALL5-PZu4[/url] Probably the best representation of what the movie was going to be like for the fans back then. Are there anymore like these, because I'm genuinely interested in how they marketed this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Mon May 05, 2014 11:03 pm This might seem a little weird and out of nowhere, but what's honestly the most creepy thing about the spot for me was when you see that shot of Misato at the door, the shot from her and Shinji's final scene together. It's weird cause, in the scene, her pushing Shinji into the elevator, with the door closing between them, was supposed to represent their separation and ultimately her death, but in the tv spot, it's in reverse, with that same door instead opening to reveal Misato, so it's like she's... coming back from the dead. it's a little weird but fucking creepy as hell to me jesus christ

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sat May 10, 2014 5:12 pm I feel like this was a fair warning to everyone how this was going to end. No one should have thought any different lol
summary:
[]: End of Eva TV Spot             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Mon Apr 28, 2014 8:59 pm Found this little gem on youtube the other day [url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4HALL5-PZu4[/url] Probably the best representation of what the movie was going to be like for the fans back then. Are there anymore like these, because I'm genuinely interested in how they marketed this movie.
